source.title: Fire Incident at Ship Shoal Block 80 Platform A

context.region: Gulf of America, Ship Shoal Block 80

actions_taken: Remaining flame extinguished with wheeled fire extinguisher
Two injured personnel evacuated via helicopter to hospital
Both individuals transported to burn unit for treatment
BSEE Incident Follow-Up Inspection conducted March 10, 2025
event.summary: On March 7, 2025, at 0815 hours, a flash fire occurred at W&T Offshore's Ship Shoal Block 80 Platform A while attempting to bring Pipeline Pump #2 online. When the electrician opened the start valve to supply fuel gas to the pump's starter, a backfire occurred followed by a flash fire. Investigation revealed an extremely loose fitting on the vent exhaust piping from the fuel gas starter allowed gas to seep out and accumulate. The backfire ignited the accumulated gas, causing a flash fire that injured a mechanic and electrician with burns to their heads and hands. Neither worker was wearing proper PPE (gloves). Wind conditions (25-30 knots from the south) pushed flames into the mechanic positioned on the northern side of the pump, causing more severe injuries. Both were evacuated and treated at burn unit, with the electrician returning to work April 9, 2025.
recommendations: When using fuel gas to start prime movers, always ensure the starter exhaust gas is vented to a safe location
Review gas engine startup procedures to include equipment checks verifying proper installation of components
Utilize gas detectors when performing startup procedures on engines with gas as a fuel source and/or as a pneumatic supply for starters
Conduct mandatory checks on engines with gas as a fuel source and/or as a pneumatic supply for starters to ensure proper installation of starter exhaust piping
Share findings at Field Lead Meeting
Conduct blitzes/audits on gas-operated equipment on all facilities to verify that all flexible hoses are replaced with hard piping
Begin routine checks on all fittings associated with starter gas
Stress the importance of wearing all required PPE
Generate safety alert to discuss the importance of inspecting equipment prior to start up
key_phrases: loose fitting on vent exhaust piping
flexible hoses instead of rigid steel pipe

threats.name: Loss of containment from loose fitting on starter exhaust piping
Flammable gas accumulation in work area

consequences.name: Personnel injury - burns to head and hands

controls.name: Periodic tightening and inspection of fittings on fuel gas systems
controls.barrier_role: Prevent loss of containment from loose fittings
controls.lod_basis: Routine maintenance to prevent equipment degradation

supporting_text: BSEE Investigators then identified an extremely loose connection/fitting on the vent exhaust piping coming from the Pipeline Pump #2's fuel gas starter
Equipment Failure: Inadequate Equipment Maintenance- The incident would have been prevented had the equipment been maintained by periodically tightening fittings
Prior to job inspect equipment/personnel: Routine checks on all fittings associated with starter gas would have prevented the incident

moving forward, when trouble shooting and/or attempting to start up natural gas-operated equipment, and particularly on equipment that has been down for an extended period of time, it is a best practice to check all hoses and fittings for cracks, corrosion, or loose fittings
review gas engine startup procedures to include equipment checks verifying proper installation of components
conduct mandatory checks on engines with gas as a fuel source and/or as a pneumatic supply for starters to ensure proper installation of starter exhaust piping

controls.name: Hazard analysis and risk assessment for gas engine startup
controls.barrier_role: Identify and control hazards before work begins
controls.lod_basis: Planning and hazard identification before task execution

supporting_text: Management Systems: No/Inadequate hazard analysis- Personnel failed to identify the hazard associated with gas accumulation in an area where a likely gas engine backfire could occur
Human Performance Error- Personnel were not aware of the hazards of loose fittings and the resulting release of and accumulation of gas and the combined hazards associated with backfires

controls.name: Gas detection during startup procedures
controls.barrier_role: Detect flammable gas accumulation before ignition
controls.lod_basis: Detection and warning system to identify hazardous conditions

supporting_text: utilize gas detectors when performing startup procedures on engines with gas as a fuel source and/or as a pneumatic supply for starters

controls.name: Proper venting of starter exhaust gas to safe location
controls.barrier_role: Prevent gas accumulation in work area
controls.lod_basis: Design control to eliminate hazard at source

supporting_text: when using fuel gas to start prime movers, always ensure the starter exhaust gas is vented to a safe location
W&T personnel pointed out that at the time of the incident, the wind was blocked by Pipeline Pump #2, which allowed hazardous gas accumulation on the downwind side where the Mechanic was crouched
both the fuel gas inlet and vent exhaust piping consisted of flexible hoses

controls.name: Use of rigid steel piping instead of flexible hoses for fuel gas systems
controls.barrier_role: Prevent fitting loosening and gas leaks
controls.lod_basis: Design control to prevent equipment failure

supporting_text: W&T management, who were present for the purpose of their own internal investigation, commented to the BSEE Investigators that they would be making recommendations to route all fuel gas through rigid steel pipe instead of a flexible hose
conduct blitzes/audits on gas-operated equipment on all W&T facilities to verify that all flexible hoses are replaced with hard piping

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) - gloves
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to protect personnel from consequences

supporting_text: W&T verified that the IP's were not following proper PPE guidelines at the time of the incident, and that neither of the IP's were wearing gloves
The burns also would have been less severe or possibly prevented had the IP's worn the proper gloves for the job being performed
Supervision- Not ensuring the Proper Use of PPE. If the IP's would have been wearing the proper PPE at the time, they may have suffered less significant injuries
stress the importance of wearing all required PPE moving forward

controls.name: Facility shutdown system
controls.barrier_role: Isolate fuel source and prevent escalation
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response system to limit consequences

supporting_text: The facility was immediately shut-in and the remaining flame was extinguished
he immediately went to notify the production operator to shut-in the facility
He reported that he immediately ran and got with the Production Operator and assisted in shutting in the facility
He confirmed that all processes were shut and secure

controls.name: Fire extinguisher and firefighting equipment
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response equipment to control consequences

supporting_text: The two then grabbed a wheeled fire extinguisher unit and put out the remaining flame on the starter
He then grabbed the nearest fire extinguisher, a rolling wheel unit, and extinguished the remaining flames

controls.name: Emergency evacuation and medical response
controls.barrier_role: Minimize severity of injuries through rapid medical treatment

supporting_text: Both individuals were evacuated via crew change helicopter from the facility to Lady of the Sea Hospital, then transported to University Hospital Burn Unit for further treatment
He immediately initiated radio communication with the nearest manned facility Ship Shoal 28 to arrange a medivac for the IP's to be transported to the onshore
They then assisted the two IP's in being transported away from the facility

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
